frame_analyses.description: Initial state: blue pegs arranged on triangular board with colored rings on right side. Instruments visible entering frame.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task setup visible; instruments positioned for initial grasp.

frame_analyses.description: Left instrument grasping first ring (purple/red) at non-dominant side. Instrument approach is controlled.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth pick-up motion; appropriate grasper positioning on ring.

frame_analyses.description: First ring in mid-air transfer from left instrument toward right grasper. Hand-off sequence initiated.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean instrument exchange; ring remains stable in grasp.

frame_analyses.description: Right instrument placing first ring on blue peg. Placement appears secure and centered on peg.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good peg alignment; ring slides onto peg smoothly.

frame_analyses.description: Second ring (red/orange) being grasped by left instrument from right collection area.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent approach technique; ring selected cleanly.

frame_analyses.description: Multiple rings now visible on pegs (yellow, red visible on pegs). Operator mid-transfer of additional rings.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Steady rhythm established; multiple rings successfully transferred.

frame_analyses.description: Task transitioned to red board (reverse phase). Instruments approaching rings on pegs for return transfer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth transition to reverse direction; instrument positioning correct.

frame_analyses.description: Suture/trace pattern task visible on pink/red surface. This indicates transition or secondary task element.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pattern tracing shows high precision and control.

frame_analyses.description: Complex knot pattern visible on pink surface with blue suture. Multiple throws and loops evident.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Detailed pattern tracing demonstrates advanced dexterity.

frame_analyses.description: Final suture pattern nearly complete on green surface. Complex intracorporeal-style knot pattern visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pattern complexity suggests advanced skill; excellent instrument control throughout.

frame_analyses.description: Video shows completion of complex suture/knot pattern on green surface with blue suture material.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task appears substantially complete with clean pattern execution.

penalties.description: Extended duration in reverse_transfer phase (99s) suggests secondary task complexity or transition element not standard to Task 1 peg transfer
penalties.note: Video transitions to suture/knot pattern task; unclear if this is continuation of Task 1 or separate task demonstration

cannot_determine: Whether all 6 rings were successfully returned to original positions in reverse phase — video transitions to different task board
Exact final completion status of reverse phase — camera switches to suture/knot task before return phase fully visible
Whether any dropped objects occurred outside frame during transitions

score_components.caveat: Score is based only on observable Task 1 peg transfer phase. Video transitions to additional task (suture/knot pattern) that extends beyond standard Task 1 completion.

confidence_rationale: Peg transfer phase was executed cleanly with no visible dropped objects or misplacements. However, video significantly extends beyond typical Task 1 duration and transitions to suture/knot pattern task (possibly Task 5 or instructional content), making completion time assessment ambiguous. Reverse phase completion not fully visible. If this is Task 1 only, score of 149 is conservative and represents intermediate performance. If additional task is included, classification and scoring become unclear.
technique_summary: Forward peg transfer phase demonstrates expert-level technique with smooth instrument exchanges and secure placements. Reverse phase begins appropriately but extends into secondary task (suture/knot pattern tracing) not standard to Task 1. Operator shows excellent dexterity and control throughout.
strengths: Smooth, efficient ring pickup from non-dominant side
Clean instrument hand-off technique in midair
Accurate, centered peg placement with no dropped objects
Excellent overall instrument control and precision
improvement_suggestions: Complete reverse phase more efficiently to achieve expert-level timing (under 60s total)
